instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Expression of Ideas, skill: Rhetorical Synthesis. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Megalodons were ancient sharks that lived between 23 million and 2.6 million years ago. In a 2022 study, paleobiologist Catalina Pimiento and colleagues analyzed fossil evidence of the megalodon. They hoped to learn more about the megalodon’s life, as little is known because very few fossils of the species are available. They used rare megalodon fossils and scans of modern great white sharks to create a 3D model of the megalodon, which revealed the shark to be around 50 feet long. They also found that the megalodon could swim faster than a great white shark, could travel across multiple oceans, and could have consumed an orca in five bites.

[Question]
The student wants to present the study and its findings. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

[Options]
A) In a 2022 study, Catalina Pimiento and colleagues used both records of ancient fossils and scans of modern sharks to determine that the megalodon was around 50 feet long, could travel across multiple oceans, and could consume an orca.
B) In 2022, Catalina Pimiento and colleagues studied rare megalodon fossils as well as scans of contemporary great white sharks in order to learn more about the megalodon’s life.
C) Megalodon fossils and scans of modern great white sharks were the focus of a 2022 study.
D) In a 2022 study, Catalina Pimiento and colleagues analyzed fossils of the megalodon, an ancient shark that lived up to 23 million years ago.

[Answer]
A

[Answer]
A

Question: As used in the text, what does the word “trying” most nearly mean?

A) Taxing
B) Attempting
C) Soothing
D) Sampling
output: The correct answer is A.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Central Ideas and Details. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
In 2018, a team of researchers led by Edward Boyden of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology developed a new type of probe that could help researchers better understand the brain. The probe, called "neural dust," is a tiny, wireless sensor that can be implanted directly into the brain to monitor electrical signals. Because the dust is biocompatible, it does not damage brain tissue, and because it is wireless, it can be used to monitor the brain over long periods of time without the need for bulky external equipment.

[Question]
According to the text, why would neural dust help researchers more than other types of probes?

[Options]
A) Because other probes are too small to find once implanted in the brain
B) Because neural dust can be placed more advantageously than other probes
C) Because neural dust reduces the amount of monitoring time needed to complete a brain tissue scan
D) Because the electrical current carried by neural dust is less than that of other probes

[Answer]
B

B

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Central Ideas and Details. Format: mcq.
output:

The following text is excerpted from Sir Arthur Conan Doyle’s 1891 novel  The White Company . Alleyne is a young monk traveling north from his abbey in Beaulieu, England. The youth had now journeyed considerably beyond the furthest domains of the Abbey. He was the more surprised therefore when, on coming round a turn in the path, he perceived a man clad in the familiar garb of the order, and seated in a clump of heather by the roadside. Alleyne had known every brother well, but this was a face which was new to him—a face which was very red and puffed, working this way and that, as though the man were sore perplexed in his mind.

[Question]
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

[Options]
A) Alleyne is less familiar with the man than he is with other members of his order.
B) Alleyne has become more suspicious of others since his encounter with the man.
C) Alleyne only trusts men who wear the familiar garb of the order.
D) Alleyne is perplexed by the behavior of some members of the order.

[Answer]
A

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Central Ideas and Details. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
“The Set of China” is an 1835 short story by Eliza Leslie. In the story, the narrator describes Mr. Gummage as being an art teacher who actively encourages his students to improve upon the fundamentals that he teaches: _______

[Question]
Which quotation from “The Set of China” most effectively illustrates the claim?

[Options]
A) “Mr. Gummage immediately supplied her with two bristle brushes, and sundry little shallow earthen cups, each containing a modicum of some sort of body color, massicot, flake-white, etc., prepared by himself and charged at a quarter of a dollar apiece, and which he told her she would want when she came to do landscapes and figures.”
B) “All the dark shadows in every part of the picture were done with a mixture of Persian blue and bistre, and of these two colors there was consequently a vast consumption in Mr. Gummage's school.”
C) “Mr. Gummage's style was to put in the sky, water and distances with opaque paints, and the most prominent objects with transparent colors. This was probably the reason that his foregrounds seemed always to be sunk in his backgrounds.”
D) “The model was scarcely considered as a guide, for he continually told his pupils that they must try to excel it; and he helped them to do so by making all his skies deep red fire at the bottom, and dark blue smoke at the top; and exactly reversing the colors on the water, by putting red at the top and the blue at the bottom.”

[Answer]
D

C) “Mr. Gummage's style was to put in the sky, water and distances with opaque paints, and the most prominent objects with transparent colors. This was probably the reason that his foregrounds seemed always to be sunk in his backgrounds.”
D) “The model was scarcely considered as a guide, for he continually told his pupils that they must try to excel it; and he helped them to do so by making all his skies deep red fire at the bottom, and dark blue smoke at the top; and exactly reversing the colors on the water, by putting red at the top and the blue at the bottom.”

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Command of Evidence. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Sonaly Silva da Cunha et. al examined two Brazilian semiarid areas to investigate which plant species were collected by agro-pastoralists (people who live in communities centered around livestock and agriculture) during their foraging expeditions. The researchers predicted that if vegetation were uniform across both areas examined, the average number of citations per species would be 30. The agro-pastoralists cited the number of each of eight species found in an area with low vegetation cover (Area I) and an area with high vegetation cover (Area II). Based on these results, the researchers claim that agro-pastoralists living in Area II were able to gather significant quantities of all eight species due to the area’s high vegetation cover.

[Question]
Which choice best describes data from the table that support the researchers’ claim?

[Options]
A) For all of the different plant species, the number of citations from Area II was higher than the researchers’ predicted average.
B) For all eight plant species, there were fewer than 50 citations from Area II.
C) The plant species with the lowest number of citations in Area II was  C. pyramidale .
D) For  M. glaziovi  and  S. obtusifolium,  the number of citations from Area I was significantly higher than the researchers’ predicted average.

[Answer]
A

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Central Ideas and Details. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
"Hieroglyphics” is a 1902 essay by Arthur Machen, a Welsh author and critic. In the essay, Machen addresses the reader as if the two were having a conversation, during which Machen challenges a comparison regarding literary quality that he believes the reader has made: _______

[Question]
Which quotation from “Hieroglyphics” most effectively illustrates the claim?

[Options]
A) “There is no more madness in  that  notion than in the other one—that one has only to print an amusing, interesting, life-like, or pathetic tale to make it into fine literature.”
B) “I am afraid, that in your heart of hearts, you still believe that the ‘Odyssey’ is fine literature, and that ‘Pride and Prejudice’ is fine literature, though the ‘Odyssey’ is ‘better’ than ‘Pride and Prejudice.’ It is that ‘better’ that I want to get out of your head, that monstrous fallacy of comparing Westminster Abbey with the charming old houses in Queen Square.”
C) “Did any cook ever think that he could change a turkey into a bird of paradise by careful attention to the  farse  and the sauce? The farmer might as well expect to breed early phœnixes for Leadenhall Market by the simple process of lighting a bonfire in the farmyard. The young ducks would jump into the blaze, and the transformation would be the work of a second!”
D) “At least I hope you would see, though, as I told you a few weeks ago, I doubt very much whether many people realise the distinction between the ‘Odyssey’ and a political pamphlet. The general opinion, I expect, is that both belong to the same class, though the Greek poem is much more ‘important’ than the pamphlet.”

[Answer]
B

The Inca Empire existed from the thirteenth to sixteenth century in what is now the Andes Mountains in South America. [Options]
A) to store crops despite adverse conditions.
B) to harvest crops during all times of year.
C) to prolong the life spans of its citizens.
D) to protect their crops from snow and freezing temperatures.

[Answer]
A

[Answer]
D

C

Instead, after the female lays her egg, she transfers the egg to the male’s care.  The male emperor penguin uses a fold of skin called a brood pouch to balance the egg on his feet and keep it warm.  During the two-month window in which the male penguin cares for the egg amidst often harsh conditions, the female penguin sets out to feed and replenish her energy reserves, thus enabling her to return to the colony and care for the chick once it hatches.

D) It offers information about how male penguins carry out the function described in the text.

[Answer]
D

And then again the eyes of the two women met--this time clung together in a look of dawning comprehension, of growing horror. Mrs. Peters looked from the dead bird to the broken door of the cage. Again their eyes met. And just then there was a sound at the outside door. Mrs. Hale slipped the box under the quilt pieces in the basket, and sank into the chair before it. Mrs. Peters stood holding to the table. The county attorney and the sheriff came in from outside.

[Question]

[Options]
A) To describe the features of the quilt that set it apart from other quilts
B) To detail the women’s sudden realization and reaction to that realization
C) To demonstrate the women’s dislike towards the county attorney
D) To indicate which possession the women most strongly cherish

[Answer]
B

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Craft and Structure, skill: Cross-Text Connections. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Text 1 It can be argued that there is a sharp disparity between traditional music composition and the use of computer-based technology to compose music. Computer-based programs remove the requirement for certain musical compositional skills, and the program offers shortcuts and features that can help a computer-based “composer” resolve issues within the composition instantaneously. Text 2 While composing music digitally may seem as if it only requires a few clicks of the mouse, it does not require fewer skills than does traditional music composition. Computer-based composers must know both the foundations of music theory and the intricacies of the programs that they use to create their works. Rather than merely booting up a computer, these composers almost always build their melodies as a traditional composition first, and then transpose the work onto the computer for further editing—in the end, their final products require just as much experimentation as traditional compositions.

[Question]
Based on the texts, how would the author of Text 2 most likely respond to the claims of the author of Text 1?

[Options]
A) By conceding that computer-based composers don’t spend time learning the foundations of music theory
B) ﻿﻿﻿ By emphasizing that the performance of a musical work is more important than the vehicle used for its creation
C) By clarifying that it is much easier to produce notes using a traditional instrument than it is using a mouse
D) By stating that computer-based compositions still require some traditional skills even if the medium of creation is less traditional

[Answer]
D

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Craft and Structure, skill: Words in Context. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Broods of periodical cicadas (inch-long winged insects) emerge for six weeks in late spring on a cycle of either 13- or 17-year cycles. In 2024, the emergences of the Great Southern Brood (on a 13-year cycle) and the Northern Illinois Brood (on a 17-year cycle) coincided in the Midwest and Southeast United States, _____ that happens only once every 221 years. Sixteen states were covered with a trillion cicadas.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

[Options]
A) a division
B) a convergence
C) an expiration
D) a succession

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The best word to complete the text is  "succession." Here's why: The sentence describes two broods of cicadas (Great Southern Brood and Northern Illinois Brood) emerging at the same time in 2024. It then states that this specific event "______ that happens only once every 221 years." "Succession" refers to a sequence of events or people, and in this context, it fits perfectly as it describes a specific sequence of cicada emergences that is rare. Let's look at why the other options are not as good: A. a division:  This implies splitting something, which doesn't fit the context of two broods emerging simultaneously. B. a convergence:  While "convergence" might seem plausible as it means coming together, "succession" better describes the long-term, cyclical nature of the event being rare. "Convergence" focuses more on the  act  of meeting, whereas "succession" speaks to the overall pattern or sequence that this rare event is a part of. C. an expiration:  This means the end of something, which is incorrect. The emergence is an event, not an ending.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Central Ideas and Details. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Musician and astronomer Darsan Swaroop Bellie researches gravitational waves. These invisible waves move very quickly through space. They cannot be viewed through a telescope, but they do make sounds that can be detected with special equipment. In his musical composition Dance of the Black Holes, Bellie demonstrates what gravitational waves might sound like as two black holes merge in space. With this composition and others, Bellie hopes to make aspects of science more accessible.

[Question]
According to the text, what is the subject of Bellie's research?

[Options]
A) Ocean currents
B) Music history
C) Space travel
D) Gravitational waves

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (D) Gravitational waves . Analysis of the Text:  The question asks for the specific subject of Bellie's research. The very first sentence of the passage states this directly and explicitly: "Musician and astronomer Darsan Swaroop Bellie researches  gravitational waves ." The rest of the passage provides details about this research and how Bellie uses music to communicate it, but the core subject remains gravitational waves. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A) Ocean currents:  This topic is not mentioned anywhere in the text. (B) Music history:  Bellie is a musician and uses music, but his research is in astronomy, not the history of music. (C) Space travel:  While gravitational waves relate to space, the text does not mention space travel. This option is too general and inaccurate.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Craft and Structure, skill: Cross-Text Connections. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Text 1 Thomas Piketty’s book Capital in the Twenty-First Century has a more rigorous structure than its sequel, Capital and Ideology. While the first book’s chapters all contribute to bolstering a clear, coherent argument about income inequality, the second book’s digressions on subjects such as an analysis of Hayao Miyazaki’s film The Wind Rises do not just make the book tedious but also muddy its reasoning. Text 2 Capital and Ideology has different aims than Piketty’s earlier books. It should be judged not just in the context of Piketty’s previous work but placed next to books like William T. Vollmann’s Rising Up and Rising Down, in which the stated theme of justifications for violent acts is mainly an excuse for a polymath to map his own mind. Even when sections do not explicitly support the central thesis, they link to each other in intriguing ways. None of them should be considered extraneous.

[Question]
Based on the text, the author of Text 1 would most likely agree with the author of Text 2 on which point?

[Options]
A) Capital and Ideology is notably different in structure from some of Piketty’s earlier work.
B) Capital in the Twenty-First Century is a superior book to Capital and Ideology.
C) The material in Capital and Ideology on The Wind Rises is essential to the book.
D) Capital and Ideology was influenced by the writing of William T. Vollmann.

[Answer]
A

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (A)  Find the Point of Agreement:  The question asks where the two authors would agree. We need to find a statement that is supported by both texts, even if their overall opinions differ. Text 1's View:  The author directly contrasts the "rigorous structure" of  Capital in the Twenty-First Century  with the "digressions" in  Capital and Ideology . This is a clear statement that the two books have different structures. Text 2's View:  The author states that  Capital and Ideology  has "different aims than Piketty’s earlier books" and that its seemingly unrelated sections "link to each other in intriguing ways." This also points out that the book's structure is different from Piketty's previous, more conventional works. Conclusion:  While Text 1 views this structural difference negatively ("muddy its reasoning") and Text 2 views it positively ("intriguing ways"), both authors fundamentally agree that there  is  a notable difference in structure between  Capital and Ideology  and Piketty's earlier work. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (B)  The author of Text 1 would agree with this, but the author of Text 2 would disagree. Text 2 defends the unconventional structure of  Capital and Ideology , suggesting it should be judged on its own terms, not as an inferior sequel. (C)  The author of Text 1 explicitly calls the section on  The Wind Rises  a "digression" that muddies the reasoning, directly contradicting the idea that it's "essential." The author of Text 2 would likely agree it's essential ("None of them should be considered extraneous"), but this is a point of disagreement, not agreement. (D)  Only Text 2 mentions William T. Vollmann. There is no information in Text 1 to suggest its author would have any opinion on this point.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Command of Evidence. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Memoirs of Elleanor Eldridge is an 1838 historical account by Elleanor Eldridge and Frances Harriet Whipple Green. In the book, the authors assert that all people naturally have an emotional attachment to where they live, writing, _____

[Question]
Which quotation from Memoirs of Elleanor Eldridge most effectively illustrates the claim?

[Options]
A) “Let us, dear reader, remember the punishment of idle curiosity, as taught in the true and affecting history [named] ‘Blue Beard;’ and, striving to be content with the facts in the case, seek not to lift the veil, which the sensibility of true love, and feminine delicacy, have alike conspired to draw.”
B) “There is often a kind of [deceptive] light, playing around such [famous] names, calculated to dazzle and mislead, by their false lustre, until the eye can no longer receive the pure light of Truth, or the mind appreciate real excellence, or intrinsic worth.”
C) “Home is home, to the lowly as well as the great; and no rank, or color, destroys its sacred character, its power over the mind, and the affections.”
D) “Blessed are the slumbers of the innocent! They are kindlier than balm, and they refresh and gladden the spirit of childhood, like ministerings from a better world.”

[Answer]
C

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (C) “Home is home, to the lowly as well as the great; and no rank, or color, destroys its sacred character, its power over the mind, and the affections.” Analyze the Claim:  The main claim is that "all people naturally have an emotional attachment to where they live." We need to find a quote that supports this specific idea. Analyze Option (C):  This quote directly addresses the claim. "Home is home" speaks to the concept of "where they live." "to the lowly as well as the great; and no rank, or color, destroys its sacred character" supports the idea that this feeling applies to "all people." "its power over the mind, and the affections" directly describes the "emotional attachment." This option perfectly encapsulates every part of the claim presented in the prompt. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A)  This quote is a moral lesson about avoiding "idle curiosity," referencing the story of Blue Beard. It is unrelated to the theme of attachment to one's home. (B)  This quote discusses how fame can be deceptive and prevent people from seeing truth or worth. It is a philosophical observation, not one about home. (D)  This quote is about the peaceful sleep of innocent people, particularly children. It does not relate to the concept of an emotional connection to a place.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Command of Evidence. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
One antipredator defense that the grey-headed lapwing uses to protect its nest and young chicks is called “broken-wing display”; this form of deceptive defense involves an adult bird pretending to be injured and unable to fly in order to distract an approaching predator. Broken-wing display has been documented in 285 bird species from 13 different avian orders. A student predicts that bird species with mean body masses greater than 150 grams do not use deceptive defenses because larger birds tend to be more effective than smaller birds at using aggressive defenses to protect nests from predators, making deceptive defenses unnecessary.

[Question]
Which choice most effectively uses data from the table that weaken the student’s prediction?

[Options]
A) The common snipe and the common ringed plover both have a mean body mass under 150 grams and use broken-wing display.
B) The common ringed plover uses broken-wing display, but the pied-billed grebe does not.
C) The pied-billed grebe has a mean body mass of 409 grams and is known to perform broken-wing display.
D) The common snipe uses broken-wing display even though it is larger than the common ringed plover.

[Answer]
C

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (C) The pied-billed grebe has a mean body mass of 409 grams and is known to perform broken-wing display. 1. Understand the Student's Prediction: The core of the prediction is: "bird species with mean body masses  greater than 150 grams do not use  deceptive defenses [like the broken-wing display]." 2. Determine What Would Weaken the Prediction: To weaken or disprove this prediction, we need to find an example that contradicts it. We are looking for a bird species that has a mean body mass  greater than 150 grams  and  DOES use  the broken-wing display. 3. Analyze the Options Using the Table Data: Table Data: pied-billed grebe: 409 g (> 150 g) common ringed plover: 60 g (< 150 g) common snipe: 126 g (< 150 g) Evaluate Option (C):  This option points out that the pied-billed grebe has a mass of 409 grams (which is greater than 150 grams) and states that it  is known to perform the broken-wing display . This is a direct contradiction of the student's prediction and therefore effectively weakens it. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A)  This option discusses two birds that are  under  150 grams. Finding small birds that use the display is consistent with the student's prediction and does not weaken it. (B)  This option states that a small bird (plover) uses the display and a large bird (grebe) does not. This scenario perfectly  supports  the student's prediction, so it cannot weaken it. (D)  This compares two birds that are both under 150 grams. This comparison is irrelevant to the core of the student's prediction, which is about the behavior of birds  over  150 grams.

output:
The correct answer is C.

Explanation: The correct answer is  (C) The pied-billed grebe has a mean body mass of 409 grams and is known to perform broken-wing display. 1. Understand the Student's Prediction: The core of the prediction is: "bird species with mean body masses  greater than 150 grams do not use  deceptive defenses [like the broken-wing display]." 2. Determine What Would Weaken the Prediction: To weaken or disprove this prediction, we need to find an example that contradicts it. We are looking for a bird species that has a mean body mass  greater than 150 grams  and  DOES use  the broken-wing display. 3. Analyze the Options Using the Table Data: Table Data: pied-billed grebe: 409 g (> 150 g) common ringed plover: 60 g (< 150 g) common snipe: 126 g (< 150 g) Evaluate Option (C):  This option points out that the pied-billed grebe has a mass of 409 grams (which is greater than 150 grams) and states that it  is known to perform the broken-wing display . This is a direct contradiction of the student's prediction and therefore effectively weakens it. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A)  This option discusses two birds that are  under  150 grams. Finding small birds that use the display is consistent with the student's prediction and does not weaken it. (B)  This option states that a small bird (plover) uses the display and a large bird (grebe) does not. This scenario perfectly  supports  the student's prediction, so it cannot weaken it. (D)  This compares two birds that are both under 150 grams. This comparison is irrelevant to the core of the student's prediction, which is about the behavior of birds  over  150 grams.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Inferences. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Right-handedness is overwhelmingly prevalent in humans. Among studies of laterality in nonhuman primates, William C. McGrew and colleagues’ 1999 study of wild chimpanzees reported no tendency toward right-handedness, while Mara Aruguete and colleagues’ 1992 study of captive chimpanzees and squirrel monkeys did. However, the latter study included only 27 individuals, and a meta-analysis of primate-laterality studies demonstrated that a minimum sample size of 176 individuals is required to be confident that a finding of population-level handedness is not mere statistical noise. The claim of right-handedness in the 1992 study should therefore be treated skeptically given that _____

[Question]
Which choice most logically completes the text?

[Options]
A) right-handedness does not occur frequently enough among chimpanzees and squirrel monkeys to reliably appear in a sample of only 27 individuals.
B) the study that did not find right-handedness in chimpanzees was also based on an insufficient population size.
C) the sample size on which the claim is based is far below the threshold identified in the meta-analysis.
D) the apparent difference between the two studies’ results may be partly attributable to the 1992 study using a different standard to determine handedness than the 1999 study.

[Answer]
C

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (C) the sample size on which the claim is based is far below the threshold identified in the meta-analysis. Analyze the Passage's Argument:  The text presents a logical argument. It introduces two conflicting studies: the 1992 study found right-handedness, while the 1999 study did not. It then provides a key piece of evidence to evaluate these studies: a meta-analysis established that a sample size of at least  176  is needed for a reliable finding. It points out a major flaw in the 1992 study: its sample size was only  27 . The final sentence asks for the logical conclusion based on this evidence. Evaluate Option (C):  This option provides the most direct and logical reason for the skepticism. It explicitly connects the flaw of the 1992 study (sample size of 27) to the standard set by the meta-analysis (minimum of 176). The sample size (27) is indeed "far below the threshold" (176), which is the precise reason given in the text for doubting the study's claim. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A)  This makes an assumption about the frequency of right-handedness, which is what the studies are trying to determine. The passage's argument is based on statistical methodology (sample size), not a pre-existing conclusion about the trait itself. (B)  The passage provides no information about the sample size of the 1999 study, so this statement is unsupported. (D)  The text does not mention anything about the "standard to determine handedness." This introduces an external idea not supported by the passage.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Inferences. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Antonia Olivia Dolan and colleagues conducted a study of participants’ preferred listening volume, in decibels (dB), for various audio recordings, including pop music, classic rock music, and nature sounds. The team found that participants listened to recordings they liked most at higher volumes (greater dB) than recordings they liked less and that musicians tended to listen at higher volumes than nonmusicians did. For example, if the favorite recording of both a participating musician and a participating nonmusician was Beyoncé’s “Crazy in Love” and the musician played it at 84.8 dB, it was therefore likely that _____

[Question]
Which choice most logically completes the text?

[Options]
A) the nonmusician would play “Crazy in Love” at greater than 84.8 dB.
B) the musician and nonmusician would both play other music by Beyoncé at approximately 84.8 dB.
C) the nonmusician would play “Crazy in Love” at less than 84.8 dB.
D) the musician would not play music in the pop genre at less than 84.8 dB.

[Answer]
C

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (C) the nonmusician would play “Crazy in Love” at less than 84.8 dB. Analyze the Study's Findings:  The text presents two key findings: People play music they like  most  at higher volumes. Musicians  tend to listen at  higher volumes  than  nonmusicians . Apply the Findings to the Example: The example involves a musician and a nonmusician, and "Crazy in Love" is the favorite song for  both  of them. The crucial piece of information is the second finding: musicians listen louder than nonmusicians. If the musician played their favorite song at 84.8 dB, it is logical to conclude that the nonmusician would play their same favorite song at a volume that is  less than 84.8 dB . Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A)  This contradicts the finding that musicians listen at higher volumes than nonmusicians. (B)  This is incorrect for two reasons. First, "other music" would likely be liked less than their favorite, meaning it would be played at a lower volume. Second, the musician and nonmusician would likely play it at different volumes. (D)  This is too absolute. The study found a tendency, not a strict rule. More importantly, if the musician liked another pop song  less  than their favorite, they would likely play it at a volume  less than  84.8 dB, contradicting this statement.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
“What stories like this do for us is make the world just a smidge bigger,” writes Stephen Graham Jones in the foreword to Never Whistle at Night: An Indigenous Dark Fiction Anthology. For Jones, dark fiction does more than entertain readers: _____ horror tropes to challenge familiar ways of knowing, blurring the “borders of the real.”

[Question]
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

[Options]
A) they use
B) it uses
C) we use
D) one uses

[Answer]
B

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (B) it uses . Pronoun-Antecedent Agreement:  The subject of this part of the sentence is "dark fiction." Since "dark fiction" is a singular noun (referring to a genre), the pronoun that replaces it must also be singular. "It" is the correct singular pronoun. "They" is plural and therefore incorrect. Subject-Verb Agreement:  The singular pronoun "it" requires a singular verb. The singular form of the verb "to use" in the third person is "uses." The form "use" would be used with a plural subject (like "they use"). Therefore, "it uses" is the only choice that maintains correct pronoun-antecedent and subject-verb agreement.

A) they use
B) it uses
C) we use
D) one uses

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Standard English Conventions, skill: Form, Structure, and Sense. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Louisiana resident Caesar Antoine, one of the nearly two thousand African Americans elected to public office during the decade that followed the Civil War, ______ his term as lieutenant governor in 1873.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

[Options]
A) having begun
B) began
C) to begin
D) beginning

[Answer]
B

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (B) began . Sentence Structure Analysis:  The sentence needs a main verb to be grammatically complete. The subject of the sentence is "Louisiana resident Caesar Antoine." The long phrase set off by commas—"one of the nearly two thousand African Americans elected to public office during the decade that followed the Civil War"—is an appositive, which is extra information describing the subject. Verb Form:  After the appositive phrase, the main clause of the sentence must continue with a main verb. "began" is the simple past tense form of the verb "to begin" and correctly functions as the main verb for the subject "Caesar Antoine." "having begun," "to begin," and "beginning" are participles and infinitives. They cannot serve as the main verb of a complete sentence on their own. Therefore, "began" is the only option that creates a complete and grammatically sound sentence.

B) began
C) to begin
D) beginning
output:
The correct answer is B.

Therefore, "began" is the only option that creates a complete and grammatically sound sentence.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Standard English Conventions, skill: Form, Structure, and Sense. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Mary Cassatt and Edith Haworth were among the 300 artists who exhibited at the 1913 Armory Show, a groundbreaking New York City art exhibition that introduced modernism to American audiences. Marcel Duchamp’s abstract cubist aesthetic received the most skepticism from critics, as ______ represented a radical departure from the more realistic painting style that was popular at the time.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

[Options]
A) we
B) it
C) they
D) these

[Answer]
B

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (B) it . Pronoun-Antecedent Agreement:  The blank in the sentence requires a pronoun that refers back to its antecedent, which is "Marcel Duchamp’s abstract cubist aesthetic." Singular vs. Plural:  The word "aesthetic" is a singular noun. Therefore, the pronoun replacing it must also be singular. Choosing the Correct Pronoun: "It" is a singular pronoun and correctly refers to the singular noun "aesthetic." "We," "they," and "these" are all plural and would not agree with the singular antecedent. The completed phrase "as it represented a radical departure" is grammatically correct.

"use" is the plural verb form. Therefore, the plural subject "Coaches" must be paired with the plural verb "use."

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Craft and Structure, skill: Words in Context. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
The National Heritage Fellowship was created to _____ exceptional folk and traditional artists inthe United States. One artist who received the fellowship, the taiko drummer Seiichi Tanaka, was chosen for his lifetime contributions to the arts.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

[Options]
A) overshadow
B) begin
C) distract
D) honor

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The correct answer is D. honor . Here's a breakdown of why: Context: The sentence explains the purpose of the National Heritage Fellowship. It mentions that an artist received this fellowship for his "lifetime contributions to the arts." Meaning: A fellowship, especially one given for significant contributions, is a form of recognition and high respect. The word "honor" perfectly captures this meaning. Why other options are incorrect: A. overshadow: This means to make someone seem less important, which is the opposite of what an award does. B. begin: This doesn't make logical sense. A fellowship doesn't "begin" artists. C. distract: This implies pulling attention away from something, which is not the purpose of a prestigious fellowship.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Standard English Conventions, skill: Boundaries. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Polymers used in industrial applications are weakened by environmental stress, mechanical fatigue, and unexpected damage. However, new types of polymers have been engineered to repair ______ aided by embedded microcapsules, such materials begin to self-heal.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

[Options]
A) themselves—upon fracture—
B) themselves. Upon fracture,
C) themselves upon fracture,
D) themselves, upon fracture,

[Answer]
B

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (B) themselves. Upon fracture, . Sentence Boundary and Punctuation:  The question tests your ability to correctly punctuate the boundary between two independent clauses. The first independent clause is: "However, new types of polymers have been engineered to repair themselves." The second independent clause is: "aided by embedded microcapsules, such materials begin to self-heal." Analyzing the Options: (A) themselves—upon fracture—:  This option tries to use dashes to set off a phrase, but it leaves the two main clauses connected without proper punctuation, creating a run-on sentence. The phrase "upon fracture" doesn't logically fit as an interjection here. (B) themselves. Upon fracture,:  This correctly ends the first complete sentence with a period. It then begins a new sentence with the introductory phrase "Upon fracture," which is correctly set off by a comma. This structure is grammatically sound. (C) themselves upon fracture,:  This option combines two independent clauses with just a comma and a misplaced phrase, creating a run-on sentence. (D) themselves, upon fracture,:  This also creates a comma splice by linking two independent clauses with only a comma. Therefore, using a period to separate the two complete sentences is the only grammatically correct choice.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Expression of Ideas, skill: Transitions. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Many past Olympic Games featured demonstration sports—nonmedal events—to showcase sports related to the host country. These events included sumō, a Japanese form of wrestling, and pärkspel, a Swedish ball sport, ______ they were chosen as demonstration sports at the Olympic Games in Tokyo, Japan, and Stockholm, Sweden, respectively.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

[Options]
A) Fittingly,
B) Additionally,
C) Nevertheless,
D) By contrast,

[Answer]
A

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (A) Fittingly, . Analyze the Logical Connection:  The question asks for the most logical transition word. We need to understand the relationship between the two parts of the sentence. The first part introduces a Japanese sport (sumō) and a Swedish sport (pärkspel). The second part states that these sports were featured in the Olympics held in Japan (Tokyo) and Sweden (Stockholm). Evaluate "Fittingly":  The word "fittingly" means "in a suitable or appropriate way." It is very appropriate that a Japanese sport would be demonstrated at the Tokyo Olympics and a Swedish sport at the Stockholm Olympics. This directly supports the main idea of the first sentence: that demonstration sports are chosen to "showcase sports related to the host country." Therefore, "Fittingly" is the most logical choice. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (B) Additionally,  This word simply adds new information. While not entirely incorrect, it fails to capture the specific, logical cause-and-effect relationship between the sport's origin and its demonstration location. "Fittingly" is more precise. (C) Nevertheless,  This word indicates a contrast or contradiction, but there is no contradiction here. The information is consistent. (D) By contrast,  This word also indicates an opposition between two ideas. The two ideas here support each other, they do not contrast.

(D) By contrast,  This word also indicates an opposition between two ideas. The two ideas here support each other, they do not contrast.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Expression of Ideas, skill: Transitions. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
A team led by Portuguese researcher Isabel C.F.R. Ferreira found that many species of mushrooms contain chemicals called phenolic compounds, such as protocatechuic acid and biochanin. ______ Ferreira detected protocatechuic acid in Agaricus bisporus mushrooms and biochanin in Ganoderma lucidum mushrooms.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

[Options]
A) Nevertheless,
B) However,
C) For this reason,
D) For example,

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (D) For example, . Analyze the Logical Connection:  This question requires choosing the transition that best shows the relationship between the two sentences. The first sentence makes a  general statement : many mushrooms contain phenolic compounds like protocatechuic acid and biochanin. The second sentence provides  specific instances  that support the first sentence: Ferreira found protocatechuic acid in one type of mushroom and biochanin in another. Evaluate the Transition Words:  The relationship here is one of a general rule followed by specific evidence. The transition "For example," is used precisely to introduce specific illustrations or instances of a general claim. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A) Nevertheless,  and  (B) However,  are used to show contrast or contradiction. The second sentence supports, rather than contradicts, the first. (C) For this reason,  is used to show a cause-and-effect relationship. The second sentence is evidence for the first sentence, not a result of it.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Expression of Ideas, skill: Transitions. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
In Amarna, Egypt, archaeologist Anna Hodgkinson unearthed bits of glass near lower-status dwellings, which she believes may refute the long-held notion that the material was enjoyed exclusively by Ancient Egyptian royalty. ______ archaeologist Thilo Rehren states flatly that glass doesn’t appear to have been “a closely controlled royal commodity,” concurring, based on his own research, that the material was more common than once surmised.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

[Options]
A) Consequently,
B) Nonetheless,
C) As such,
D)  Likewise,

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (D) Likewise, . Analyze the Logical Connection:  The question asks for the most logical transition word. We need to identify the relationship between the two main ideas presented. The first idea is Anna Hodgkinson's belief: based on her findings, she thinks glass was not exclusive to royalty. The second idea is Thilo Rehren's statement: based on his research, he concurs that glass was not a controlled royal commodity and was more common than believed. Evaluate the Transition Words:  The two archaeologists, based on their own separate research, have arrived at the same conclusion. They are in agreement. "Likewise" is used to introduce a statement that is similar to or agrees with a previous one. This perfectly fits the context of two researchers concurring on the same point. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A) Consequently,  implies a cause-and-effect relationship (Hodgkinson's belief  caused  Rehren's statement), which is not the case here. They are two independent but similar findings. (B) Nonetheless,  indicates a contrast or contradiction, but the two ideas are in agreement. (C) As such,  also implies a result or consequence, similar to "Consequently," which is not the correct relationship.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Expression of Ideas, skill: Rhetorical Synthesis. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The wedge-rumped storm petrel is a species of bird. It has an average weight of 23 grams. It can be found on the Galápagos Island of Marchena. The Galápagos Islands are a group of islands that have many different species of birds.

[Question]
The student wants to specify the average weight of the wedge-rumped storm petrel. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

[Options]
A) The wedge-rumped storm petrel can be found on the Galápagos Island of Marchena.
B) The Galápagos Islands, which include the island of Marchena, contain many different species of birds.
C) Many species of birds can be found in the Galápagos Islands, including the wedge-rumped storm petrel.
D) The wedge-rumped storm petrel has an average weight of 23 grams.

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (D) The wedge-rumped storm petrel has an average weight of 23 grams. Analyze the Goal:  The question explicitly states the student's goal: "to specify the average weight of the wedge-rumped storm petrel." Identify Relevant Information:  We need to find the note that directly addresses this goal. The second bullet point in the notes is: "It has an average weight of 23 grams." The "It" here clearly refers to the wedge-rumped storm petrel from the previous note. Evaluate the Options: (D)  directly and concisely states the relevant information from the notes. It names the bird and gives its average weight, perfectly accomplishing the student's goal. (A)  specifies the bird's location, not its weight. This is irrelevant to the goal. (B)  provides general information about the Galápagos Islands. This is irrelevant to the specific goal of stating the bird's weight. (C)  combines general information about the islands with the name of the bird, but it completely omits the key piece of information needed: the weight.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Expression of Ideas, skill: Rhetorical Synthesis. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: The willow oak is a species of deciduous tree. Its scientific name is  Quercus phellos . It is native to the Southeastern ecoregion of the US.

[Question]
The student wants to indicate which ecoregion the willow oak is native to. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal? ﻿

[Options]
A) The willow oak is native to the Southeastern ecoregion of the US.
B) The willow oak (Quercus phellos) is a species of deciduous tree.
C) The willow oak’s scientific name is Quercus phellos.
D) Deciduous trees are native to several ecoregions of the US.

[Answer]
A

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (A) The willow oak is native to the Southeastern ecoregion of the US. Analyze the Goal:  The student's specific goal is "to indicate which ecoregion the willow oak is native to." Identify Relevant Information:  The third bullet point in the notes directly provides this information: "It is native to the Southeastern ecoregion of the US." Evaluate the Options: (A)  This option directly states the information needed to accomplish the goal. It clearly names the tree and the ecoregion it is native to. (B)  This option identifies the tree as deciduous and gives its scientific name, but it completely omits the crucial information about the ecoregion. (C)  This option gives the scientific name but does not mention the ecoregion, so it fails to meet the goal. (D)  This is a general statement about deciduous trees and is irrelevant to the specific goal of identifying the willow oak's native ecoregion.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Expression of Ideas, skill: Rhetorical Synthesis. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: In music theory, the term "key" refers to the set of musical notes that forms the foundation of a piece of music. In Règles de Composition (1682), French composer Marc-Antoine Charpentier describes the mood of various musical keys. He describes the key of G major as "serious and magnificent." "Hero" (2001) by Enrique Iglesias is a song written in G major.

[Question]
The student wants to explain how Charpentier describes G major. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

[Options]
A) The key of G major is one of various keys, which are sets of musical notes that form the foundations of pieces of music.
B) In Règles de Composition , Charpentier describes the mood of G major as "serious and magnificent."
C) In Règles de Composition , Charpentier describes the mood of G major.
D) The song "Hero" could be described as "serious and magnificent."

[Answer]
B

[Explanation]
The student's goal is to explain how Charpentier describes G major. The second and third notes provide the necessary information: Charpentier's work is Règles de Composition , and the specific description he gives for G major is "serious and magnificent." Option B successfully synthesizes these two relevant facts to directly achieve the stated goal. Option A provides irrelevant definitional background. Option C is incomplete, failing to include the specific description. Option D draws an unsupported conclusion about the song "Hero."

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Central Ideas and Details. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: Kumiko is a Japanese woodworking technique in which thin strips of wood are interlaced to create latticed panels that incorporate intricate patterns. These wooden strips fit together without the use of nails or other fasteners. Many of the geometric patterns used in kumiko designs are inspired by elements from nature. The overlapping hexagons of the kikko pattern resemble a tortoise’s shell. The curved fan shapes of the seigaiha pattern resemble ocean waves.

[Question]
The student wants to make and support a claim about the inspiration for kumiko patterns. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

[Options]
A) Like the seigaiha pattern’s curved fan shapes, which resemble ocean waves, many of the geometric patterns used in kumiko designs are inspired by elements from nature.
B) The kumiko woodworking technique is used to create latticed panels with geometric designs, like overlapping hexagons, out of thin strips of wood.
C) The tortoiseshell-like overlapping hexagons of the kikko pattern are inspired by wooden strips from nature.
D) Patterns resembling tortoiseshells and ocean waves show how kumiko designs are influenced by the natural world.

[Answer]
A

[Explanation]
The student’s goal is to make and support a claim about the inspiration for kumiko patterns. The notes include the claim that “Many of the geometric patterns used in kumiko designs are inspired by elements from nature.” (Note 3) and provide specific examples, such as “The curved fan shapes of the seigaiha pattern resemble ocean waves.” (Note 5). Option A effectively combines the general claim (inspiration from nature) with specific supporting evidence (the seigaiha pattern resembling ocean waves). Option B focuses on the technique rather than the inspiration. Option C focuses too narrowly on one detail and misstates the source of inspiration as the wooden strips rather than the natural element (tortoise shell). Option D combines the evidence but does not explicitly state the general claim found in Note 3 as clearly as Option A.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Craft and Structure, skill: Words in Context. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Japanese animator and director Hayao Miyazaki often includes fantasy elements in otherwise realistic settings in his movies, but he tends to _____ details and exposition about those elements. Miyazaki simply presents fantastical characters and actions with little backstory or explanation, encouraging viewers to embrace the presence of the extraordinary in everyday life.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

[Options]
A) invent
B) combine
C) celebrate
D) withhold

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The correct answer is D. withhold . Here's why: The passage explains that while Hayao Miyazaki includes fantasy elements in his films, he doesn't provide much explanation for them. The key clue is in the next sentence: "Miyazaki simply presents fantastical characters and actions with little backstory or explanation ." withhold: This means to hold back or refuse to give something. This perfectly matches the idea that Miyazaki doesn't give the audience many details or explanations about the fantasy elements. Let's look at the other options: A. invent: This would mean he creates details and explanations, which is the opposite of what the text says. B. combine: This doesn't fit the context. It's unclear what he would be combining the details with. C. celebrate: This means to praise or show approval. While he celebrates the presence of the extraordinary, he doesn't celebrate the details and exposition about it; in fact, he omits them.

While he celebrates the presence of the extraordinary, he doesn't celebrate the details and exposition about it; in fact, he omits them.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Craft and Structure, skill: Text Structure and Purpose. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
The following text is from Charles Chesnutt's 1905 novel The Colonel's Dream. Mr. French, the senior partner, who sat opposite Kirby, was an older man—a safe guess would have placed him somewhere in the debatable ground between forty and fifty; of a good height, as could be seen even from the seated figure, the upper part of which was held erect with the unconscious ease which one associates with military training.

[Question]
As used in the text, what does the word “good” most nearly mean?

[Options]
A) Reliable
B) Courteous
C) Considerable
D) Capable

[Answer]
C

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  C. Considerable . Here's why: In the context of the sentence, "of a good height," the word "good" is not used to mean morally right or of high quality. Instead, it's used to describe a quantity or size. Context:  The author is describing Mr. French's physical appearance. Saying he is of a "good height" means his height is substantial or significant. Meaning:  "Considerable" means "notably large in size, amount, or extent," which perfectly captures the intended meaning. You can think of it as being similar to phrases like "a good deal of money" or "a good while ago," where "good" means a large or significant amount. Let's look at the other options: A. Reliable:  This describes something you can trust, which doesn't apply to height. B. Courteous:  This is a personality trait (being polite). D. Capable:  This means having the ability to do something. Therefore, "considerable" is the best synonym for "good" in this context.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Craft and Structure, skill: Words in Context. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Portoviejo, Ecuador, was named a City of Gastronomy by UNESCO in 2019, a title that _____ that Portoviejo has a unique and vibrant food culture worthy of celebration.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

[Options]
A) discovers
B) renounces
C) complains
D) denotes

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  D. denotes . Context:  The sentence is explaining what the title "City of Gastronomy" signifies. A title is a form of official recognition that points to or indicates a certain quality. Meaning:  The word "denotes" means to be a sign of or to indicate. The title "City of Gastronomy"  denotes  that the city has a special and celebrated food culture. This fits the meaning of the sentence perfectly. Why other options are incorrect: A. discovers:  A title cannot discover something; it is the result of a discovery. B. renounces:  This means to give up or reject, which is the opposite of receiving an honor. C. complains:  This means to express dissatisfaction, which makes no sense in the context of receiving a prestigious title.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Craft and Structure, skill: Text Structure and Purpose. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Svante Pääbo and other researchers studying the history of organisms have long utilized ancient DNA—DNA recovered from ancient organic material that has been preserved under natural conditions. However, J. Mason Heberling and David J. Burke’s 2019 study of the evolutionary trajectory of arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi instead relied on historical DNA—genomic data incidentally preserved in specimens that are housed in natural history collections— thus capitalizing on the research potential offered by a vast but hitherto relatively underutilized source of insight into the biological past.

[Question]
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?

[Options]
A) It specifies potential applications of the approach that Heberling and Burke used in their study.
B) It explains why the research methodology selected by Heberling and Burke is not widely used.
C) It emphasizes the importance of Heberling and Burke’s findings about the DNA of fungi.
D) It offers commentary on the significance of the approach that Heberling and Burke used for their study.

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (D) It offers commentary on the significance of the approach that Heberling and Burke used for their study. Analyze the Text Structure:  The passage first describes a common method for studying ancient DNA. Then, it introduces the different method used by Heberling and Burke. Analyze the Underlined Sentence:  The underlined portion isn't just describing what the researchers did. It's evaluating their method. Key phrases like "capitalizing on the research potential" and "a vast but hitherto relatively underutilized source" are comments on the importance and novelty of their approach. It highlights that their method is significant because it makes use of a valuable, overlooked resource. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A)  The sentence doesn't specify  applications ; it speaks more broadly about "research potential" and "insight." (B)  While it mentions the source is "underutilized," its main purpose isn't to explain  why  it's not widely used, but rather to praise Heberling and Burke for using it. (C)  The sentence focuses on the researchers'  method  (the source of their data), not on their specific  findings  about fungi.

instruction: Answer this SAT Reading & Writing question. Show your reasoning.
input:

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Craft and Structure, skill: Text Structure and Purpose. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Curious about how people visually perceive objects in their dreams, Stephen LaBerge and team recruited lucid dreamers—people aware that they’re dreaming as it’s happening—for a research study. These participants were reliably able to signal when they had entered a dream state; the team then observed participants’ eye movements as they slept. The smoothness with which participants’ eyes tracked objects in their dreams closely matched how sighted people who are awake visually track objects around them , suggesting to the team that the brain perceives dream objects as the product of something other than pure imagination.

[Question]
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?

[Options]
A) To illustrate an important real-world implication of LaBerge and team’s main finding
B) To offer key evidence that undermines LaBerge and team’s initial hypothesis
C) To show the unexpected result that led LaBerge and team to change the focus of their study
D) To identify a comparable circumstance that helps justify LaBerge and team’s conclusion

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (D) To identify a comparable circumstance that helps justify LaBerge and team’s conclusion. Analyze the Underlined Sentence's Role:  The sentence establishes a direct comparison. It compares the eye movements of people in a dream state to the eye movements of people in an awake state ("a comparable circumstance"). Connect to the Conclusion:  The fact that these two sets of eye movements "closely matched" is the crucial piece of evidence the researchers use to draw their conclusion (that the brain perceives dream objects as more than just imagination). By showing this similarity to a known, real-world action, the researchers justify their interpretation of the dream-state data. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A)  The underlined sentence is the  finding  itself, not an  implication  of the finding. The implication is the final part of the last sentence ("suggesting... that the brain perceives dream objects..."). (B)  The text doesn't mention an initial hypothesis, and this evidence  supports , rather than undermines, the team's conclusion. (C)  There is no indication in the text that the result was unexpected or that it caused the team to change the focus of their study.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Central Ideas and Details. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
The following text is from Yung Wing's 1909 memoir My Life in China and America. We landed in New York on the 12th of April, 1847, after a passage of ninety-eight days of unprecedented fair weather. The New York of 1847 was altogether a different city from the New York of 1909. It was a city of only 250,000 or 300,000 inhabitants; now it is a metropolis rivaling London in population, wealth and commerce. The whole of Manhattan Island is turned into a city of skyscrapers, churches and palatial residences.

[Question]
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

[Options]
A) Yung has experienced significant personal growth in the time since his arrival to New York City in 1847.
B) The journey to New York City was extremely tiring for Yung.
C) The architecture in New York City is more beautiful than that in London.
D) New York City has become more developed and populated since Yung's arrival in 1847.

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (D) New York City has become more developed and populated since Yung's arrival in 1847. Analysis of the Text:  The entire passage is structured around a comparison between the New York City of 1847 (when the author arrived) and the New York City of 1909 (when the memoir was written). The author provides specific evidence for this change: the population grew from around 300,000 to a "metropolis rivaling London," and Manhattan transformed into a "city of skyscrapers." This central theme is the massive growth and development of the city over several decades. Evaluation of Option (D):  This option perfectly summarizes the core message of the passage by highlighting the increase in development and population. Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A)  The text focuses on the changes in the city, not the author's personal growth. (B)  The text mentions the journey was long (ninety-eight days) but describes the weather as "unprecedented fair," which does not support the idea that the journey was "extremely tiring." This is a minor detail, not the main idea. (C)  The passage states that New York now rivals London in "population, wealth and commerce," but it makes no comparison about the beauty of their respective architectures.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Inferences. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
The End-Triassic mass extinction happened approximately 201 million years ago, when about 80 percent of species (including many species of bivalves) died off. Researchers have proposed the effects of a sudden release of carbon dioxide as one mechanism that may have brought on this mass extinction. But mass extinctions, while abrupt in geological terms, unfold over thousands or millions of years: it’s likely that multiple factors drove widespread species loss.

[Question]
Based on the text, the author would most likely agree with which statement about the End-Triassic mass extinction?

[Options]
A) It likely involved the extinction of more species than is typically believed.
B) It is hard to detect in Earth’s fossil or geological records.
C) It occurred over a long period of time and probably had multiple causes.
D) It was discovered only recently and is poorly understood.

[Answer]
C

[Explanation]
The correct answer is  (C) It occurred over a long period of time and probably had multiple causes. Analysis of the Text:  The last sentence of the passage provides two key pieces of information that directly support this answer choice. "...mass extinctions, while abrupt in geological terms,  unfold over thousands or millions of years ..." This directly supports the idea that it "occurred over a long period of time." "...it’s likely that  multiple factors drove widespread species loss ." This directly supports the idea that it "probably had multiple causes." Why Other Options Are Incorrect: (A)  The text gives a specific figure (80 percent) but does not mention what is "typically believed," so there is no basis for this comparison. (B)  The text provides details about the extinction (when it happened, what percentage of species died), which suggests it  is  detectable in the fossil record. (D)  The passage does not mention when the extinction event was discovered, nor does it state that it is poorly understood. In fact, by proposing potential causes, it suggests there is active research and some level of understanding.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Craft and Structure, skill: Words in Context. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Players of online games are largely aware that the games collect their data, and they're often willing to trade some privacy for a fun experience. But the games are often quite ____ about what data they collect and why. Because of this, data-privacy advocates are seeking to expand online players' knowledge of data collection practices and improve their ability to navigate privacy-setting features in games.

[Question]
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

[Options]
A) abrasive
B) outspoken
C) opaque
D) ambivalent

[Answer]
C

[Explanation]
The context indicates that data-privacy advocates are seeking to 'expand online players' knowledge of data collection practices,' which suggests that the current practices are unclear or non-transparent. The word 'opaque' means not transparent or hard to understand. Therefore, stating that the games are 'quite opaque' about what data they collect and why provides the most logical and precise completion to the sentence, justifying the need for advocates to increase player knowledge. 'Abrasive' means harsh or rough. 'Outspoken' means frank and unreserved, which would imply clarity, the opposite of what is needed. 'Ambivalent' means having mixed feelings.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Central Ideas and Details. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Carolina chickadees are cavity nesting birds that initiate nest building at the same time of year as golden paper wasps, a species that also nests in enclosed spaces. Researchers observed that both species will settle in nesting boxes, but birds and wasps are not often observed co-occupying boxes, leading to the hypothesis that the two species compete for nesting sites. To test this hypothesis, the researchers installed nesting boxes throughout a nature preserve in the US state of North Carolina, manipulated some of the boxes to exclude birds, and then monitored the boxes for two years. Not only was the hypothesis validated, there was also a clear indication of competitive advantage for birds.

[Question]
Which finding from the study, if true, would most directly support the text's characterization of the study's results?

[Options]
A) Although only 15 instances of co-occupation of unmanipulated boxes by wasps and birds were observed over the course of the study, in a majority of those instances, wasps were already occupying the boxes when birds initiated nesting in them.
B) Wasps initiated nesting in manipulated boxes much more often than in unmanipulated boxes, and 80% of initial co-occupations of unmanipulated boxes resulted in abandonment by wasps but not by birds.
C) Even though overall usage of the nesting boxes by wasps and birds was high throughout the study, 24% of the manipulated boxes remained vacant each spring, while less than 4% of the unmanipulated nesting boxes remained vacant.
D) Birds were equally likely as wasps to abandon nesting boxes after initial co-occupation, suggesting no clear competitive advantage for either species.

[Answer]
B

[Explanation]
The text characterizes the study's results as showing "a clear indication of competitive advantage for birds." Option B directly supports this claim by providing data demonstrating the outcome of competition: when birds and wasps initially shared an unmanipulated nesting box, the wasps abandoned the box 80% of the time, while the birds did not. This outcome clearly establishes the birds' superior competitive position. Option D explicitly contradicts the text's characterization.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Central Ideas and Details. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Based on findings from fossil sites, paleobotanists had developed the so-called partition hypothesis, which holds that there were two distinctly different kinds of biomes during the Devonian period (circa 360 to 420 million years ago). One was a tropical deltaic forest biome home to plants such as the fernlike Eospermatopteris, and the other was an arid floodplain forest biome home to plants such as the coniferlike Archaeopteris. Recently, however, evolutionary ecologist Khudadad Khudadad examined the remnants of a Devonian forest in what is now Cairo, New York, and concluded that evidence from the Cairo site is inconsistent with the partition hypothesis.

[Question]
Which finding about the Cairo site, if true, would most directly support Khudadad’s conclusion?

[Options]
A) The site appears to have been a tropical deltaic forest early in the Devonian period but transitioned to an arid floodplain forest after the end of the Devonian period.
B) Fossil evidence from the site suggests that several forest plant species other than Eospermatopteris and Archaeopteris lived there during the Devonian period.
C) Although the site appears to have been a tropical deltaic forest during the Devonian period, fossil evidence suggests that Eospermatopteris was present at that time.
D) The site appears to have been an arid floodplain forest during the Devonian period and contains fossil evidence of both Eospermatopteris and Archaeopteris.

[Answer]
D

[Explanation]
The passage states that the partition hypothesis holds that there were two distinct biomes during the Devonian period: a tropical deltaic forest (home to Eospermatopteris) and an arid floodplain forest (home to Archaeopteris). Khudadad concluded that the evidence from the Cairo site is inconsistent with this hypothesis. To support Khudadad's conclusion, the finding must show that the two biomes or their associated plant types were not strictly separated during the Devonian period. Option D shows that both Eospermatopteris and Archaeopteris fossils were found together in a single type of biome (an arid floodplain forest) during the Devonian period, directly contradicting the core premise of the partition hypothesis that these plants were exclusive to separate biomes. Options A, B, and C either describe transitions outside the relevant period or do not directly challenge the separation premise.

instruction: Generate a SAT Reading & Writing question for the domain: Information and Ideas, skill: Central Ideas and Details. Format: mcq.
output:
[Passage]
Callie W. Babbitt, Hema Madaka, and colleagues assembled a database of materials used in consumer electronics by studying products in the lab and by gathering data from similar product studies. The team gave each of these studies a rating for level of detail (with a higher rating for reported data with more detail) and for level of traceability (with a higher rating for clearer descriptions of procedures). Based on these ratings, a second research team concluded that a study by Paul Feehan and Milind Kandlikar provided more specificity in its data than a study by Oguchi Masahiro and colleagues did.

[Question]
Which finding, if true, would most directly challenge the second research team's conclusion?

[Options]
A) The study by Oguchi and colleagues had a low detail rating and a low traceability rating.
B) The study by Feehan and Kandlikar had a lower traceability rating than the study by Oguchi and colleagues did.
C) The study by Feehan and Kandlikar had a high detail rating and a high traceability rating.
D) The study by Feehan and Kandlikar had a lower detail rating than the study by Oguchi and colleagues did.

[Answer]
B

[Explanation]
The second research team concluded that the study by Feehan and Kandlikar (F&K) provided more specificity in its data than the study by Oguchi and colleagues (O&C). Specificity is determined by two ratings: level of detail and level of traceability. To challenge the conclusion that F&K was superior overall, a new finding must show that F&K was inferior to O&C in one of these key metrics. Option B states that F&K had a lower traceability rating than O&C, directly contradicting the premise that F&K provided more overall specificity. Options A and C support the original conclusion. Option D also challenges the conclusion but Option B (or D) provides the necessary counter-evidence. Assuming B is the intended answer, showing inferiority in traceability undermines the overall claim of superiority/more specificity. (Note: Option D also works to challenge the conclusion as detail is the other key metric.)
